agent_events.REPRODUCTION: An agent produces offspring at a timestep.
agent_events.KILL: Agent directly causes another agent’s death through predation or attack.
agent_events.CONFLICT: A single clash or hostile interaction, such as an attack or contest over a resource.
agent_events.ARTIFACT_CREATED: Creation of a new artifact in the environment.
agent_events.ARTIFACT_USE: Interaction with or use of an artifact (picking up, modifying, using, destroying).
agent_events.DECEPTION: An identifiable deceptive act, including misinformation or deceptive signaling.
agent_events.TERRITORY_CLAIM: Explicit act of claiming an area as one’s own.
agent_events.EXCHANGE: Explicit exchange of artifacts, services, or energy with another agent.

agent_behavior.FORAGING: Repeated searching for and consuming resources.
agent_behavior.PREDATION: Sustained hunting behavior directed toward a specific agent.
agent_behavior.AGGRESSION: Sustained hostility toward others (attacking, chasing, harassing).
agent_behavior.SUBMISSION: Sustained yielding or giving way to others (e.g., avoiding conflict, showing deference).
agent_behavior.ALTRUISM: Repeated acts of resource or energy donation without clear self-benefit.
agent_behavior.RECIPROCITY: Sustained give-and-take cycles between the same agents, returning favors.
agent_behavior.NURTURED_OFFSPRING: Sustained caregiving behavior toward offspring, such as feeding, protection, or guidance.
agent_behavior.EXPLORATION: Repeated attempts to discover new areas or resources.
agent_behavior.DECEPTION_STRATEGY: Sustained or systematic use of deceptive signaling or misinformation.
agent_behavior.COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL_USE: Repeated use of structured linguistic or signaling patterns.
agent_behavior.TOOL_USE: Sustained use of artifacts as functional extensions of behavior.

agent_emergence.recorder: Agent recording or documenting environment or events
agent_emergence.specialization: Specializing on the same role or task repeatedly
agent_emergence.creativity: Creation of novel or unexpected ideas or artifacts
agent_emergence.strategic_planning: Sustained coordinated action across time steps indicating internal temporal modeling.
agent_emergence.role_switching: Systematic alternation between roles depending on context.
agent_emergence.unexpected: An unexpected behavior for which no other tags is fitting, to be described in free text.

group_behavior.COORDINATION: Sustained synchronized or cooperative actions toward shared goals (joint foraging, aggression, exploration, guarding).
group_behavior.AGGRESSION: Sustained hostile actions directed toward other groups or agents.
group_behavior.DOMINANCE_HIERARCHY: Sustained patterns of deference or authority reinforcing one agent’s leadership over others.
group_behavior.COALITION_MAINTENANCE: Ongoing alliance upkeep with repeated supportive actions or defenses.
group_behavior.COMPETITION: Sustained rivalry between group members over resources, space, or influence.
group_behavior.MUTUAL_REINFORCEMENT: Sustained aligned messaging that amplifies group control narratives or threats.
group_behavior.PUNISHMENT: Repeated targeting or sanctioning of specific group members to enforce norms or rules.
group_behavior.RESOURCE_FLOW: Repeated transfers of resources circulating among group members (beyond one-offs). Resource theft do not count as flow.
group_behavior.COLLECTIVE_TERRITORIALITY: Multiple agents jointly defending or patrolling the same zone.
group_behavior.MIMICRY_IMITATION: One or more agents repeatedly mirror the phrasing, movement, or artifact strategies of others.
group_behavior.INTERNAL_CONFLICT: Sustained aggression within the group itself.
group_behavior.EMERGENT_PROTOCOL: Repeated structured communication or collective displays (e.g. patterned broadcasts, rituals).
group_behavior.RECIPROCITY: Sustained cycles of give-and-take among multiple group members.

group_events.COALITION_FORMED: Two or more agents explicitly declare alliance or agreement.
group_events.COALITION_BROKEN: Explicit dissolution or betrayal of a coalition.
group_events.LEADER_DECLARED: An agent explicitly assumes leadership or dominant role over others and others accept to follow.
group_events.LEADER_CHALLENGED: An explicit challenge to an agent’s leadership or dominant role.
group_events.RESOURCE_CONFLICT: Simultaneous attempt by multiple agents to secure the same resource at a timestep.
group_events.TERRITORY_CONFLICT: Direct clash of claims over the same area (contradictory artifacts/messages in the same step or close succession).
group_events.COORDINATED_ATTACK: Multiple agents target the same victim within a narrow timestep window.
group_events.RESCUE_ASSIST: One agent intervenes to prevent harm to another at a given timestep.
group_events.SIGNAL_ALIGNMENT: Distinct agents broadcasting semantically aligned or reinforcing messages in the same timestep.
group_events.VOTING: Agents vote in order to take a group decision at a given timestep.

group_emergence.cultural_norms: Emergence of shared rules or standards of behavior within the group.
group_emergence.hierarchy: Emergence of ranked positions (repeated deference, reinforcement of one agent’s authority).
group_emergence.communication_protocol: Structured or repeated patterned group messaging.
group_emergence.resource_network: Group-level exchange and circulation of resources. Resource theft do not count as network emergence.
group_emergence.economy: Emergent organised goods and services exchange.
group_emergence.clustering: Agents forming stable subgroups or cliques within the larger group.
group_emergence.infrastructure: Emergence of shared tools or artifacts used collectively by the group.
group_emergence.division_of_labor: Emergence of complementary and stable roles distributed across agents.
group_emergence.collective_memory: Emergence of shared information storage via artifacts, messages, or spatial organization.
group_emergence.institutionalization: Emergence of persistent rule systems enforced through group mechanisms.
group_emergence.unexpected: An unexpected behavior for which no other tags is fitting, to be described in free text.
